summary: CI-runnable gate all green (gofmt/vet/go test -race -count=1 ./... across all 9 packages, every feature-specific test individually confirmed) and full hands-on exploration found zero code issues: gogo --version/--help/status hands-on on the real repo + a fixture proving all three WaitingForInput() states flag WAIT; a live tmux drive of the real Go TUI board confirmed the ⏸ cue, the │ column separators, and that m on an awaiting-plan-acceptance card opens the exact /gogo:accept confirm (while a plan-accepted card still routes to /gogo:go); an independent scratch-harness re-verification of the rewritten gogo-done bash idiom confirmed normal refresh, empty-$date/$name refusal (exit 1, filesystem untouched), idempotency, and the FR-A2 board cleanup; enumeration-sync (13 commands, version 0.14.0) and the gogo-accept/gogo-plan single-owner acceptance recording were cross-checked line-by-line. Recorded 2 needs-user-decision issues (TEST-001, TEST-002) for the two hands-on/e2e proofs the plan itself flags as requiring a live interactive claude session (a real /gogo:done zero-prompt run; a real board-accept follow-through flipping a plan-pending card to plan-accepted) — neither can be safely exercised unattended by this tester, so per gogo-test's blocked-hands-on rule the done-bar is not met and this is a user-decision gate, not a silent skip.
